Haiden.JPG|270px|Haiden]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tateyama|Q646755|WD=Q646755|1=Tateyama, Chiba}} Sunomiya 921 | coordinates = {{coord|34|57|09|N|139|50|17|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hirinome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|WD=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (grand shrine) {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} | architecture = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=August 9|Q2782|WD=Q2782|1=August 9}} }} [[ファイル:洲宮神社 鳥居.JPG|220px|right|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sunomiya Shrine''' (すのみやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Sunomiya, {{ill|lt=Tateyama|Q646755|WD=Q646755|1=Tateyama, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for the Engishiki grand shrine "Ame-no-Hirinome-no-Mikoto Shrine" and was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * '''{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hirinome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|WD=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}}''' : Said to be the consort deity of {{ill|lt=Awa Shrine|Q705547|WD=Q705547|1=Awa Shrine}}'s deity and formerly called "Susa no Kami". ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}} (leader of Awa Imibe in the development of Awa) A historical issue is whether the correct reading is "Hirinome" or "Hiritome," and this has long been discussed in relation to nearby {{ill|lt=Sunosaki Shrine|Q11556513|WD=Q11556513|1=Susaki Shrine}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, when Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto developed Awa, a shrine was founded here near the sea and called "Susa-gami" or "Sunomiya." In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), the shrine appears as "Kisakigami Ame-no-Hirinome-no-Mikoto Shrine, grand, former name Susa-gami" in Awa District. Since the Edo period, Sunomiya Shrine and Sunosaki Shrine have disputed which is the Engishiki shrine. In 1872, the Ministry of Doctrine once recognized Sunomiya, then reversed the next year and recognized Sunosaki. === Cultural properties === === Tateyama City designated cultural properties === * Wooden tenbu statue (Nanbokucho to early Muromachi) * ''Sunomiya Jinja Engi'' manuscript * Earthen ritual model objects from the late Kofun period (from former site, To-yama) * Sunomiya Shrine rice-planting ritual (intangible folk cultural property, held on New Year's morning) === Site information === * Address: Sunomiya 921, Tateyama, Chiba * Access: From JR Uchibo Line Tateyama Station, JR bus toward Awa-Shirahama via Kobe; get off at "Sunomiya," then about 2 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Zenkoku Jinja Meikan'' (Vol. 1), 1977 * Fumio Kimizuka, "Sunosaki Shrine / Sunomiya Shrine" in ''Nihon no Kamigami 11: Kanto'' * ''Chusei Shokoku Ichinomiya-sei no Kiso-teki Kenkyu'', 2000 === External links === {{commonscat|Sunomiya-jinja}} * [https://tateyamacity.com/archives/2754 Sunomiya Shrine] - Tateyama Tourism Association * {{神道・神社史料集成|170201|Kisakigami Ame-no-Hirinome-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sunomiya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sunomiya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sunomiya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:館山市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Tateyama, Chiba]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Inbe clan]] [[Category:Zen Clan]][[Category:qq]]

'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sunomiya-jinja}} [[JA:洲宮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11556507}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Sunomiya Shrine | image = [[ファイル:Sunomiya-jinja Haiden.JPG|270px|Haiden]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tateyama|Q646755|WD=Q646755|1=Tateyama, Chiba}} Sunomiya 921 | coordinates = {{coord|34|57|09|N|139|50|17|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hirinome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|WD=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (grand shrine) {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} | architecture = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=August 9|Q2782|WD=Q2782|1=August 9}} }} [[ファイル:洲宮神社 鳥居.JPG|220px|right|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sunomiya Shrine''' (すのみやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Sunomiya, {{ill|lt=Tateyama|Q646755|WD=Q646755|1=Tateyama, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for the Engishiki grand shrine "Ame-no-Hirinome-no-Mikoto Shrine" and was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * '''{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hirinome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|WD=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}}''' : Said to be the consort deity of {{ill|lt=Awa Shrine|Q705547|WD=Q705547|1=Awa Shrine}}'s deity and formerly called "Susa no Kami". ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}} (leader of Awa Imibe in the development of Awa) A historical issue is whether the correct reading is "Hirinome" or "Hiritome," and this has long been discussed in relation to nearby {{ill|lt=Sunosaki Shrine|Q11556513|WD=Q11556513|1=Susaki Shrine}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, when Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto developed Awa, a shrine was founded here near the sea and called "Susa-gami" or "Sunomiya." In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), the shrine appears as "Kisakigami Ame-no-Hirinome-no-Mikoto Shrine, grand, former name Susa-gami" in Awa District. Since the Edo period, Sunomiya Shrine and Sunosaki Shrine have disputed which is the Engishiki shrine. In 1872, the Ministry of Doctrine once recognized Sunomiya, then reversed the next year and recognized Sunosaki. === Cultural properties === === Tateyama City designated cultural properties === * Wooden tenbu statue (Nanbokucho to early Muromachi) * ''Sunomiya Jinja Engi'' manuscript * Earthen ritual model objects from the late Kofun period (from former site, To-yama) * Sunomiya Shrine rice-planting ritual (intangible folk cultural property, held on New Year's morning) === Site information === * Address: Sunomiya 921, Tateyama, Chiba * Access: From JR Uchibo Line Tateyama Station, JR bus toward Awa-Shirahama via Kobe; get off at "Sunomiya," then about 2 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Zenkoku Jinja Meikan'' (Vol. 1), 1977 * Fumio Kimizuka, "Sunosaki Shrine / Sunomiya Shrine" in ''Nihon no Kamigami 11: Kanto'' * ''Chusei Shokoku Ichinomiya-sei no Kiso-teki Kenkyu'', 2000 === External links === {{commonscat|Sunomiya-jinja}} * [https://tateyamacity.com/archives/2754 Sunomiya Shrine] - Tateyama Tourism Association * {{神道・神社史料集成|170201|Kisakigami Ame-no-Hirinome-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sunomiya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sunomiya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sunomiya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tateyama, Chiba]] [[Category:Buildings in Tateyama, Chiba]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Inbe clan]] [[Category:Zen Clan]]

'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sunomiya-jinja}} [[JA:洲宮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11556507}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Sunomiya Shrine | image = [[ファイル:Sunomiya-jinja Haiden.JPG|270px|Haiden]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tateyama|Q646755|WD=Q646755|1=Tateyama, Chiba}} Sunomiya 921 | coordinates = {{coord|34|57|09|N|139|50|17|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hirinome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|WD=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (grand shrine) {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} | architecture = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=August 9|Q2782|WD=Q2782|1=August 9}} }} [[ファイル:洲宮神社 鳥居.JPG|220px|right|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sunomiya Shrine''' (すのみやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Sunomiya, {{ill|lt=Tateyama|Q646755|WD=Q646755|1=Tateyama, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for the Engishiki grand shrine "Ame-no-Hirinome-no-Mikoto Shrine" and was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * '''{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hirinome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|WD=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}}''' : Said to be the consort deity of {{ill|lt=Awa Shrine|Q705547|WD=Q705547|1=Awa Shrine}}'s deity and formerly called "Susa no Kami". ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|Q24899653|WD=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}} (leader of Awa Imibe in the development of Awa) A historical issue is whether the correct reading is "Hirinome" or "Hiritome," and this has long been discussed in relation to nearby {{ill|lt=Sunosaki Shrine|Q11556513|WD=Q11556513|1=Susaki Shrine}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, when Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto developed Awa, a shrine was founded here near the sea and called "Susa-gami" or "Sunomiya." In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), the shrine appears as "Kisakigami Ame-no-Hirinome-no-Mikoto Shrine, grand, former name Susa-gami" in Awa District. Since the Edo period, Sunomiya Shrine and Sunosaki Shrine have disputed which is the Engishiki shrine. In 1872, the Ministry of Doctrine once recognized Sunomiya, then reversed the next year and recognized Sunosaki. === Cultural properties === === Tateyama City designated cultural properties === * Wooden tenbu statue (Nanbokucho to early Muromachi) * ''Sunomiya Jinja Engi'' manuscript * Earthen ritual model objects from the late Kofun period (from former site, To-yama) * Sunomiya Shrine rice-planting ritual (intangible folk cultural property, held on New Year's morning) === Site information === * Address: Sunomiya 921, Tateyama, Chiba * Access: From JR Uchibo Line Tateyama Station, JR bus toward Awa-Shirahama via Kobe; get off at "Sunomiya," then about 2 minutes on foot === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Zenkoku Jinja Meikan'' (Vol. 1), 1977 * Fumio Kimizuka, "Sunosaki Shrine / Sunomiya Shrine" in ''Nihon no Kamigami 11: Kanto'' * ''Chusei Shokoku Ichinomiya-sei no Kiso-teki Kenkyu'', 2000 === External links === {{commonscat|Sunomiya-jinja}} * [https://tateyamacity.com/archives/2754 Sunomiya Shrine] - Tateyama Tourism Association * {{神道・神社史料集成|170201|Kisakigami Ame-no-Hirinome-no-Mikoto Shrine}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sunomiya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sunomiya Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sunomiya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tateyama, Chiba]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tateyama, Chiba]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Chiba)]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Inbe clan]] [[Category:Zen Clan]]

In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), the shrine appears as "Kisakigami Ame-no-Hirinome-no-Mikoto Shrine, grand, former name Susa-gami" in Awa District.
